fields.description:
# S2 — Auth framework + Resource-Server wiring + static-bearer verifier

## Why
This story delivers the authentication admission core — the provider-agnostic seam every
verifier plugs into — plus the first (and simplest, dependency-free) verifier so the whole path
is exercisable locally: the static bearer token (the "PAT" half of the confirmed floor). It
turns S1's HTTP transport into an *authenticated* endpoint.

## What
- Extend `McpConfig` with an `[mcp.auth]` group: `enabled` (bool, default false), `strategies`
  (ordered list selecting which verifiers are active — a composite, first-accept-wins),
  `issuer_url`, `resource_server_url`, `required_scopes`, and the static-verifier params
  (`static_tokens` source). Auto-derive `REBAR_MCP_*` env vars; update `MCP_ENV_VARS`/`server.json`.
- New module `src/rebar/_mcp_auth.py`: a `CompositeTokenVerifier` (tries each configured verifier,
  first non-None `AccessToken` wins, any error → None) and a config→verifier **factory**; the
  **`StaticBearerVerifier`** (SHA-256 hash-at-rest, `hmac.compare_digest` constant-time compare,
  per-token `client_id`/`scopes`, mandatory audience/scope handling). All implement the official
  SDK `mcp.server.auth.provider.TokenVerifier` protocol (`verify_token(token)->AccessToken|None`).
- Wire it in `build_server`: when `mcp.auth.enabled`, pass `token_verifier=CompositeTokenVerifier(…)`
  and `auth=AuthSettings(issuer_url=…, resource_server_url=…, required_scopes=…)` to `FastMCP`, so
  the SDK serves the RFC 9728 PRM document and emits 401 + `WWW-Authenticate`. Resolve S1's
  unauthenticated-HTTP guard against the real `mcp.auth.enabled`.
- **Mandatory audience validation:** the SDK does NOT validate `aud`/resource; the composite/base
  enforces it (equivalent to always-on `--oauth-strict`, RFC 8707) — a token whose audience is not
  this resource server is rejected.

## Approach & rationale
Model the framework on the SDK's own `TokenVerifier`/`AuthSettings` seam (the blessed extension
point the SDK's `simple-auth` example uses) and mirror the existing `readonly`/`allow_llm` gate
config pattern already in `McpConfig`. A composite verifier (rather than a single selected
strategy) lets a deployment accept, e.g., static PAT for CI *and* OIDC JWTs for humans
simultaneously — the GitHub model — without bespoke branching. Static bearer first because it
needs no external IdP, so it makes the end-to-end 401/200/403 path verifiable locally.

## Scope
The auth framework (composite + factory + config + PRM/401 wiring + mandatory audience check) and
the **static** verifier only. JWKS/JWT (S3), introspection (S4), trusted-proxy (S5), custom (S6)
are separate stories that register additional verifiers on this seam. A subset of the epic.
Depends on S1.

## Security
Boundary-crossing (authenticated network endpoint). Requirements enforced here: constant-time
token comparison; only a SHA-256 hash of each static token is held in memory (no plaintext
retained); tokens are never logged; audience validated on every request; fail-closed (verifier
error or misconfigured auth → deny / refuse start); insufficient scope → 403. Static tokens are
loaded from config/secret-store, documented as dev/internal-grade in S7.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `[mcp.auth]` config (`enabled`/`strategies`/`issuer_url`/`resource_server_url`/
      `required_scopes`/static-token source) exists with matching `REBAR_MCP_*` env vars in
      `MCP_ENV_VARS`/`server.json` (manifest drift gate passes).
- [ ] Integration test (tier: integration — httpx `ASGITransport` against
      the server's Streamable-HTTP ASGI app (the concrete `mcp` SDK entrypoint resolved against the pinned `mcp>=1.28,<2` at implementation time)) asserts, with static auth enabled: an unauthenticated request
      returns **401** and a `WWW-Authenticate` header carrying the RFC 9728 `resource_metadata`
      URL (observable oracle: status + header value).
- [ ] Integration test asserts the PRM document is served at
      `/.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/…` with an `authorization_servers` field
      (observable oracle: HTTP 200 + JSON field present).
- [ ] Integration test asserts a valid static token → **200** and an unknown/invalid token → 401;
      a token whose principal lacks a `required_scope` → **403** (negative controls included).
- [ ] Unit test asserts `StaticBearerVerifier` holds no plaintext token (only its hash) and uses a
      constant-time compare (observable oracle: the in-memory store contains the SHA-256 digest,
      not the token), and that a wrong-audience token is rejected (returns None).
- [ ] With `mcp.auth.enabled=false` the server behaves exactly as S1 (auth middleware absent).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.

## Compatibility
Additive; auth off by default. No change to stdio or the existing tools. Depends on S1's transport.
